summary: Adds async series-to-episode resolution for the Mixtape Play All feature so series IDs in the user's queue are converted to playable episode IDs before dispatching playback.
overview_md:
## Why

The Mixtape "Play All" CTA passes queue item IDs directly to the player, but series IDs are not playable content — the player needs an actual episode ID. Without resolution, pressing Play All on a queue containing series would either crash or silently skip those items. This PR adds a resolution layer that converts each series ID to the user's "next up" episode (from watch history) or falls back to S1E1.

## What

- **`resolveQueueForMixtape.ts`** (new, 133 lines) — core async utility that maps each queue slot: movies pass through, series resolve via `seriesHistoryQueryFn` → `getCurrentSeriesHistoryEpisode`, with an S1E1 fallback via `seriesPaginationInfoQueryFn`. Failed slots are dropped.
- **`useStartMixtapePlayback.ts`** (new, 62 lines) — React hook wrapping the resolver with deduplication guards (`inFlightRef`), unmount safety, and final dispatch of `dispatchMixtapeStart`.
- **`useMixtapeCtaKeyboard.ts`** (edited) — simplified by replacing 20+ lines of inline queue-building logic with a single call to `useStartMixtapePlayback`.
- **`mixtapeStore.ts`** (edited) — adds `originalSeriesId?: string` to `MixtapeItem` so downstream code can trace an episode back to its originating series.
- **`useAutoplayController.ts`** (edited) — comment-only clarification on the mixtape-suppression guard.

## How

`useStartMixtapePlayback` is consumed by `useMixtapeCtaKeyboard` (the Play All button handler). The hook calls `resolveQueueForMixtape`, which uses react-query's `fetchQuery` against existing query functions (`seriesHistoryQueryFn`, `seriesPaginationInfoQueryFn`) and existing utils (`getCurrentSeriesHistoryEpisode`, `getFirstEpisodeIdFromSeasonsMeta`). All slots resolve in parallel via `Promise.all`. The resolved list and `dataMap` (now carrying `originalSeriesId` for series) are dispatched into the mixtape zustand store via `dispatchMixtapeStart`. No existing downstream consumers of these query functions or the mixtape store are structurally affected — the new field is optional and additive.
